widgets_values: ### If your output video looks washed out, try adjusting the contrast level to bring it closer to your input images. A value of 1.1–1.2 usually works well.
### Here you can independently control the number of high‑noise and low‑noise steps.
### Here you can control the image size in megapixels while preserving the aspect ratio. The sides are scaled in steps of 16 pixels.
### Here you can choose the sampler.

# Wan SVI 2 Pro FLF — notes

This workflow is built around Wan 2.2 + SVI 2.0 Pro and FLF (First/Last Frame) logic, with custom nodes for segment stitching and end control.

## Core idea

- Use **SVI 2 Pro** for long, continuous motion across segments.
- Use **FLF-style end control** to hard-lock the last frame (or block of frames) of each segment.
- Use **WanCutLastSlot** to remove the last temporal slot before feeding latents into the next segment, to avoid over-constraining the very end.

## Models

This should work with your current models that already handle standard SVI 2 PRO, but I can't test every single one. Start with your own model combo, and if the results aren't what you expect, use the finetuned model this setup was built around until a more universal solution appears. Here are two options with speed LoRA baked in:

- **SFW**: [**High noise model**](https://civitai.com/models/2053259?modelVersionId=2376074)  [**Low noise model**](https://civitai.com/models/2053259?modelVersionId=2376133)

- **NSFW**: [**High noise model**](https://civitai.com/models/2053259?modelVersionId=2540892)  [**Low noise model**](https://civitai.com/models/2053259?modelVersionId=2540896)
